content: I'm so excited for my YouTube channel, I've been reading a lot of philosophy books to get inspiration for my videos. Have you heard of Jean-Paul Sartre?
Yes, I'm familiar with Jean-Paul Sartre. His existentialist ideas are fascinating. What kind of content do you plan on creating for your YouTube channel?

I want to create videos that combine my love of philosophy and salsa dancing. I've been taking classes at the local dance studio on Fridays and I think it would be great to share that with my audience.
That sounds like a unique and engaging concept. Have you considered collaborating with other dancers or philosophers to bring different perspectives to your channel?
Actually, I've been talking to my colleague, Dr. Lee, about potentially guest starring in one of my videos. She's a big fan of philosophy and has some great insights to share.
That's great! It's always good to have diverse perspectives. What's your goal for the number of subscribers you want to reach in the first year of your channel?
I'd like to reach at least 10,000 subscribers by the end of the year. I think it's achievable if I can consistently upload high-quality content and engage with my audience.
That's a challenging but realistic goal. Do you have any plans for how you'll be promoting your channel and reaching new viewers?

I plan on using social media platforms like Instagram and Facebook to promote my channel. I've also been thinking about attending some philosophy conferences and events to network with other like-minded individuals.
Networking can be a great way to get your channel noticed. Have you considered reaching out to other YouTubers in the philosophy or dance niches to collaborate or cross-promote each other's content?
That's a great idea. I've been a fan of the YouTube channel 'Philosophy Tube' and I think it would be great to collaborate with them in the future.
They have a great following and it could be a good opportunity for you to reach a wider audience. What's your favorite book by Jean-Paul Sartre and why?
My favorite book by Sartre is 'No Exit'. I love how it explores the concept of existentialism and the idea that 'hell is other people'.

That's a classic. The play is a great example of how Sartre's philosophical ideas can be applied to real-life situations. Do you have any upcoming videos or projects that you're particularly excited about?
Yes, I'm actually working on a video about the philosophy of dance and how it relates to existentialism. I'm planning on uploading it on March 15th, so be sure to check it out!
I'll make sure to keep an eye out for it. Good luck with your channel and I hope you reach your goal of 10,000 subscribers by the end of the year!

Thanks for the support! I'm looking forward to seeing how my channel grows and evolves over time. Maybe one day I'll even get to perform at the City Hospital's annual gala, that would be a dream come true.
That sounds like a great goal. I'm sure your colleagues at the hospital would love to see you perform. Keep working hard and I'm sure you'll get there someday.
